The Hawker Siddeley HS-121 Trident (originally the de Havilland DH.121 and briefly the Airco DH.121) is a British airliner produced by Hawker Siddeley.
In 1957, de Havilland proposed its DH.121 trijet design to a British European Airways (BEA) request.

objects.title: Sumo
objects.extract: Sumo (Japanese: 相撲, Hepburn: sumō; Japanese pronunciation: [ˈsɯmoː], lit. 'striking one another') is a form of competitive full-contact wrestling where a rikishi (wrestler) attempts to force his opponent out of a circular ring (dohyō) or into touching the ground with any body part other than the soles of his feet (usually by throwing, shoving or pushing him
objects.artist:
Painted by: Hibata Ōsuke 樋畑翁輔 (prepatory sketches, and possibly the scroll)

Cal

objects.title: Abalone
objects.description: Common name for a group of sea snails
objects.extract: Abalone (  or ; via Spanish abulón, from  Rumsen aulón), in singular "an abalone", are sea snails in the genus Haliotis, the only genus in the family Haliotidae. Abalone shells are distinctive for their flattened, ear-like shape, nacreous interior, and row of holes used for respiration.

objects.title: Personal computer
objects.description: Computer intended for use by an individual person
objects.extract: A personal computer (PC), or simply computer, is a computer designed for personal use. It is typically used for tasks such as word processing, internet browsing, email, multimedia playback, and gaming.

objects.title: Whale
objects.description: Informal group of large marine mammals
objects.extract: Whales are a widely distributed and diverse group of fully aquatic placental marine mammals. As an informal and colloquial grouping, they correspond to large members of the infraorder Cetacea, i.e.

objects.title: Supermodel
objects.extract: A supermodel is a highly paid fashion model who has a worldwide reputation and background in haute couture and commercial modeling. The term became popular in the 1990s.

objects.title: Harlequinade
objects.extract: Harlequinade is an English comic theatrical genre, defined by the Oxford English Dictionary as "that part of a pantomime in which the harlequin and clown play the principal parts". It developed in England between the 17th and mid-19th centuries.

objects.title: Dumbbell
objects.extract: The dumbbell, a type of free weight, is a piece of equipment used in weight training. It is usually used individually and/or in pairs, with one in each hand.

objects.title: Access control
objects.extract: In physical security and information security, access control (AC) is the action of deciding whether a subject should be granted or denied access to an object (for example, a place or a resource). The act of accessing may mean consuming, entering, or using.
objects.artist: U.S. Navy photo by Photographer’s Mate 3rd Class Yesenia Rosas

objects.title: Cherry
objects.description: Fruit of some plants of the genus Prunus
objects.extract:
A cherry is the fruit of many plants of the genus Prunus, and is a fleshy drupe (stone fruit).
Commercial cherries are obtained from cultivars of several species, such as the sweet Prunus avium and the sour Prunus cerasus.

objects.title: Davyhulme Circle War Memorial
objects.description: clock in Trafford, Greater Manchester, England, UK

objects.title: Snow globe
objects.description: Decorative water filled small glass sphere enclosing a miniaturized scene
objects.extract: A snow globe (also called a waterglobe, snowstorm, or snowdome) is a transparent sphere or spheroid, traditionally made of glass, enclosing a miniaturized scene of some sort, often together with a model of a town, neighborhood, landscape or figure. The sphere also encloses the water in the globe; the water serves as the medium through which the "snow" falls.

objects.title: Erg Chech 002
objects.description: Ancient meteorite discovered in the Erg Chech region of Algeria
objects.extract: Erg Chech 002 (EC 002) is an ancient andesite meteorite discovered in the Erg Chech region of the Sahara Desert in Algeria. The fall is believed to be fragments of a achondritic protoplanet that is over 4.566 billion years old, and is believed to be the oldest known volcanic rock on Earth.

objects.title: Dave's Luncheonette, Allentown, Pennsylvania
objects.artist: Lehigh County Tax Assessor's Office,  Allentown PA

objects.title: betel
objects.extract: Betel (Piper betle) is a species of flowering plant in the pepper family Piperaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It is an evergreen, dioecious vine, with glossy heart-shaped leaves and white catkins.

objects.title: Beer in the United States
objects.extract: In the United States, beer is manufactured in breweries which range in size from industry giants to brew pubs and microbreweries. The United States produced 196 million barrels (23.0 GL) of beer in 2012, and consumes roughly 28 US gallons (110 L) of beer per capita annually.

objects.title: Salmon as food
objects.extract: Salmon is a common food fish classified as an oily fish with a rich content of protein and omega-3 fatty acids. Norway is a major producer of farmed and wild salmon, accounting for more than 50% of global salmon production.

objects.title: Albatross
objects.extract: Albatrosses, of the biological family Diomedeidae, are large seabirds related to the procellariids, storm petrels, and diving petrels in the order Procellariiformes (the tubenoses). They range widely in the Southern Ocean and the North Pacific.

objects.title: Cereal
objects.extract: A cereal is a grass  (family Poaceae) cultivated for its edible grain. Cereals are the world's largest crops, and are therefore staple foods.

objects.title: Yam (vegetable)
objects.extract:
Yam is the common name for some plant species in the genus Dioscorea (family Dioscoreaceae) that form edible tubers (some other species in the genus being toxic).
Yams are perennial herbaceous vines native to Africa, Asia, and the Americas and cultivated for the consumption of their starchy tubers in many temperate and tropical regions.

objects.title: Surface-supplied diving
objects.description: Underwater diving breathing gas supplied from the surface
objects.extract: Surface-supplied diving is a mode of underwater diving using equipment supplied with breathing gas through a diver's umbilical from the surface, either from the shore or from a diving support vessel, sometimes indirectly via a diving bell. This is different from scuba diving, where the diver's breathing equipment is completely self-contained and there is no

objects.title: Kitten
objects.extract: A kitten is a juvenile cat. After being born, kittens display primary altriciality and are fully dependent on their mothers for survival.

objects.title: Bicycle helmet
objects.extract: A bicycle helmet is a type of helmet designed to attenuate impacts to the head of a cyclist in collisions while minimizing side effects such as interference with peripheral vision.

objects.title: Anklet
objects.extract: An anklet, also called ankle chain, ankle bracelet or ankle string, is an ornament worn around the ankle. Barefoot anklets and toe rings historically have been worn for at least over 8,000 years by girls and women in Indus Valley, in Indian subcontinent where it is commonly known as pattilu, payal, golusu and sometimes as nupur.

objects.title: Curare darts and quiver
objects.description: Curare pot, darts and quiver, huambisa (?), given by Étienne Antoine Gillet-Brez in 1917
objects.artist:
Rama

MEG — Musée d’ethnographie de Genève (official website)

objects.title: CDC 6600
objects.extract: The CDC 6600 is the flagship of the discontinued 6000 series of mainframe computer systems manufactured by Control Data Corporation. Generally considered to be the first successful supercomputer, it outperformed the industry's prior recordholder, the IBM 7030 Stretch, by a factor of three.

objects.title: Ancient Rome
objects.description: Roman civilisation from the 8th century BC to the 5th century AD
objects.extract:
In modern historiography, ancient Rome is the Roman civilisation from the founding of the Italian city of Rome in the 8th century BC to the collapse of the Western Roman Empire in the 5th century AD. It encompasses the Roman Kingdom (753–509 BC), the Roman Republic (509‍–‍27 BC), and the Roman Empire (27 BC – 476 AD) until the fall of the western empire.
Anc

objects.title: Growler (jug)
objects.description: Bottle used for selling craft beer and other drinks
objects.extract: A growler (US) () is a glass, ceramic, or stainless steel bottle used to transport draft beer. They are commonly sold at breweries and brewpubs as a means to sell take-out craft beer.

objects.title: Lathe
objects.description: Machine tool which rotates the work piece on its axis
objects.extract:
A lathe () is a machine tool that rotates a workpiece about an axis of rotation to perform various operations such as cutting, sanding, knurling, drilling, deformation, facing, threading and turning, with tools that are applied to the workpiece to create an object with symmetry about that axis. 
Lathes are used in woodturning,  metalworking, metal spinning,

objects.title: Max Holste MH.20
objects.extract: The Max Holste MH.20 was a French single-engined racing aircraft built to compete in the 1939 Coupe Deutsch de la Meurthe air race but not flying until 1941. A single example was built.

objects.title: Recumbent bicycle
objects.extract: A recumbent bicycle is a bicycle that places the rider in a laid-back reclining position, and often called a human-powered vehicle or HPV, especially if it has an aerodynamic fairing. Recumbents are available in a wide range of configurations, including: long to short wheelbase; large, small, or a mix of wheel sizes; overseat, underseat, or no-hands steering

objects.title: Children's clothing
objects.extract: Children's clothing or kids' clothing is clothing for children who have not yet grown to full height. Children's clothing is often more casual than adult clothing, fit for play and rest.

objects.title: Fire hydrant
objects.description: Connection point by which firefighters can tap into a water supply
objects.extract: A fire hydrant, fireplug, firecock (archaic), hydrant riser or Johnny Pump is a connection point by which firefighters can tap into a water supply. It is a component of active fire protection.

objects.title: Cactus
objects.description: Family of mostly succulent plants, adapted to dry environments
objects.extract: A cactus (pl.: cacti, cactuses, or less commonly, cactus) is a member of the plant family Cactaceae (), a family of the order Caryophyllales comprising about 127 genera with some 1,750 known species. The word cactus derives, through Latin, from the Ancient Greek word κάκτος (káktos), a name originally used by Theophrastus for a spiny plant whose identity is

objects.title: Matryoshka doll
objects.description: Russian nested wooden toy created in 1890
objects.extract: A matryoshka doll or matryoshka (; Russian: матрёшка), also known as a Russian stacking doll, nesting doll, or simply a Russian doll, is a set of wooden dolls of decreasing size placed one inside another. Matryoshka is a diminutive form of Matryosha (Матрёша), in turn an affectionate form of the Russian female first name Matryona (Матрёна).

objects.title: Phlebiopsis castanea
objects.artist: Miettinen O, Spirin V, Vlasák J, Rivoire B, Stenroos S, Hibbett D (2016) Polypor

objects.title: Thalurania ridgwayi
objects.extract: The Mexican woodnymph (Eupherusa ridgwayi) is a species of hummingbird in the family Trochilidae endemic to western Mexico. It lives in subtropical or tropical moist lowland/foothill forest and plantations, feeding on flower nectar and insects.

objects.title: Thurible
objects.description: Metal vessel on chains for burning incense
objects.extract: A thurible (via Old French from Medieval Latin turibulum) is a metal incense burner suspended from chains, in which incense is burned during worship services. It is used in Christian churches, including those of the Roman Catholic, Eastern Orthodox, Assyrian Church of the East, Oriental Orthodox, Lutheran and Old Catholic denominations, as well as in some Co

objects.title: Chow mein
objects.extract: Chow mein ( and ; simplified Chinese: 炒面; traditional Chinese: 炒麵; Mandarin Pinyin: chǎomiàn; Cantonese Yale: cháaumihn) is a dish of Chinese stir-fried noodles with vegetables and sometimes meat or tofu. Over the centuries, variations of chǎomiàn were developed in many regions of China; there are several methods of frying the noodles and a range of toppings

objects.title: Poetry jukebox in Náměstí Bratří Synků
Tasmanian devil
objects.extract: The Tasmanian devil (Sarcophilus harrisii; palawa kani: purinina) is a carnivorous marsupial of the family Dasyuridae. It was formerly present across mainland Australia, but became extinct there around 3,500 years ago; it is now confined to the island of Tasmania.

objects.title: Ligia oceanica
objects.extract: Ligia oceanica, the sea slater, common sea slater, or sea roach, is a species of isopod in the family Ligiidae. This isopod lives in the littoral zone and is abundant on the rocky shores of the North Sea, the western Baltic Sea, and the Atlantic coasts of Europe.

objects.title: 204 series integrated circuits
objects.description: Soviet series of hybrid RETL logic circuits

objects.title: Platform shoe
objects.description: Footwear with a thick platform sole
objects.extract: Platform shoes are shoes, boots, or sandals with a thick sole, usually in the range of 5–10 cm (2–4 in). Platform shoes may also be high heels, in which case the heel is raised significantly higher than the ball of the foot.

objects.title: Entertainment
objects.description: Activity that holds attention or gives pleasure
objects.extract: Entertainment is a form of activity that holds the attention and interest of an audience or gives pleasure and delight. It can be an idea or a task, but it is more commonly one of the activities or events developed over thousands of years specifically to engage an audience.

objects.title: Canadian Tire
objects.extract: Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited (doing business as Canadian Tire) is a Canadian retail corporation, headquartered at Canada Square in Toronto, Ontario. It operates department stores, gas stations (Canadian Tire Petroleum), and financial services (Canadian Tire Bank).

objects.title: Quick coupler
objects.description: Tool used to quickly move buckets
objects.extract: Quick couplers (also called quick hitches) are used with construction machines to allow the rapid change of working tools or buckets and attachments on the machine. They remove the need to use hammers to manually drive out and insert the mounting pins for attachments.

objects.title: Ant
objects.extract: Ants are eusocial insects of the family Formicidae and, along with the related wasps and bees, belong to the order Hymenoptera. Ants evolved from vespoid wasp ancestors in the Cretaceous period.

objects.title: Dodol
objects.extract: Dodol, also known as kalamae (Thai: กะละแม), or mont kalar mei (Burmese: မုန့်ကုလားမဲ) is a traditional sweet confection of Sri Lanka and Southeast Asia, particularly associated with Indonesia, Malaysia, Brunei, Singapore, Thailand and Myanmar. It is made by slowly simmering coconut milk and palm sugar with rice flour or glutinous rice until it thickens into

objects.title: bottle in glass
objects.description: bottle in glass, early modern period, location of discovery: Bruges, collection: Raakvlak, BR/L.V./3

objects.title: Unity candle
objects.extract: A unity candle is a candle used in a wedding ceremony to symbolize two people joining in marriage.

objects.title: Peanut
objects.description: Legume cultivated as a grain and oil crop
objects.extract: The peanut (Arachis hypogaea), also known as the groundnut, goober (US, via Kikongo), goober pea, pindar (US, via Kikongo) or monkey nut (UK), is a legume crop grown mainly for its edible seeds, contained in underground pods. It is widely grown in the tropics and subtropics by small and large commercial producers, both as a grain legume and as an oil crop.

objects.title: Dieppe
objects.description: Subprefecture and commune in Normandy, France
objects.extract: Dieppe (French pronunciation: [djɛp]; Norman: Dgieppe; prob. from Old English  dēōp or Old Norse djúpr 'deep') is a coastal commune in the Seine-Maritime department, Normandy, northern France.

objects.title: Closed-loop box reuse
objects.extract: Closed-loop box reuse, is the process by which boxes or other containers are reused many times.  It is a form of reusable packaging.
objects.artist: U.S. Air Force photo/Tech. Sgt. Shane A. Cuomo

objects.title: Supermodel
objects.extract: A supermodel is a highly paid fashion model who has a worldwide reputation and background in haute couture and commercial modeling. The term became popular in the 1990s.

objects.title: Automation
objects.description: Use of various control systems for operating equipment
objects.extract: Automation describes a wide range of technologies that reduce human intervention in processes, mainly by predetermining decision criteria, subprocess relationships, and related actions, as well as embodying those predeterminations in machines. Automation has been achieved by various means including mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic, electrical, electronic dev

objects.title: Oil platform
objects.description: Offshore ocean structure with oil drilling and related facilities
objects.extract: An oil platform, also called an oil rig, offshore platform, or oil production platform, is a large structure with facilities to extract and process petroleum and natural gas that lie in rock formations beneath the seabed. Many oil platforms will also have facilities to accommodate the workers, although it is also common to have a separate accommodation platf

objects.title: Pack animal
objects.description: Individual or type of working animal used by humans
objects.extract:
A pack animal, also known as a sumpter animal or beast of burden, is a working animal used to transport goods or materials by carrying them, usually on its back.
Domestic animals of many species are used in this way, among them alpacas, Bactrian camels, donkeys, dromedaries, gaur, goats, horses, llamas, mules, reindeer, water buffaloes and yaks.

objects.title: Electricity meter
objects.description: Device used to measure electricity use
objects.extract:
An electricity meter, electric meter, electrical meter, energy meter, kilowatt-hour meter, or power meter is a device that measures the amount of electric energy consumed by a residence, a business, or an electrically powered device over a time interval.
Electric utilities use electric meters installed at customers' premises for billing and monitoring purpos

objects.title: Korean cuisine
objects.extract: Korean cuisine is the set of foods and culinary styles which are associated with Korean culture. This cuisine has evolved through centuries of social and political change.

objects.title: Samovar
objects.description: Metal container used to heat and boil water
objects.extract: A samovar (Russian: самовар, IPA: [səmɐˈvar] , lit. 'self-brewer') is a metal container traditionally used to heat and boil water. Although originating in Russia, the samovar is well known outside of the country and has spread through Russian culture to other parts of Eastern Europe, as well as West and Central and South Asia.
objects.artist:
Dimitri.

Original uploader was Ivorymammoth at en.wikipedia

objects.title: Water heating
objects.description: Thermodynamic process that uses energy sources to heat water
objects.extract: Water heating is a heat transfer process that uses an energy source to heat water above its initial temperature. Typical domestic uses of hot water include cooking, cleaning, bathing, and space heating.

objects.title: Aviator sunglasses
objects.extract: Aviator sunglasses are a style of sunglasses that was developed by a group of American firms. The original Bausch & Lomb design is now commercially marketed as Ray-Ban Aviators, although other manufacturers also produce aviator-style sunglasses.

objects.title: Air brake (road vehicle)
objects.description: Type of friction brake for vehicles
objects.extract: An air brake or, more formally, a compressed-air-brake system, is a type of friction brake for vehicles in which compressed air pressing on a piston is used to both release the parking/emergency brakes in order to move the vehicle, and also to apply pressure to the brake pads or brake shoes to slow and stop the vehicle.  Air brakes are used in large heavy ve

objects.title: Fern
objects.extract: Ferns (Polypodiopsida or Polypodiophyta) are a group of vascular plants (land plants with vascular tissues such as xylem and phloem) that reproduce via spores and have neither seeds nor flowers. They differ from non-vascular plants (mosses, hornworts and liverworts) by having specialized transport bundles that conduct water and nutrients from and to the root

objects.title: Nyctimene albiventer
objects.extract: The common tube-nosed fruit bat (Nyctimene albiventer) is a species of megabat in the family Pteropodidae. It is found at islands north of Australia, and in Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, the Philippines and the Solomon Islands.

objects.title: Noh
objects.extract: Noh (能, Nō; Japanese pronunciation: [no(ꜜ)ː], Sino-Japanese for "ability") is a major form of classical Japanese dance-drama that has been performed since the 14th century. It is Japan's oldest major theater art that is still regularly performed today.

objects.title: Stoneware
objects.description: Pottery fired at a relatively high temperature
objects.extract: Stoneware is a broad class of pottery fired at a relatively high temperature, to be impervious to water. A modern definition is a vitreous or semi-vitreous ceramic made primarily from stoneware clay or non-refractory fire clay.

objects.title: FS 235.3005 in Pietrarsa railway museum
objects.description: locomotive in the Pietrarsa railway museum, Naples, Italy

objects.title: Dolphin
objects.description: Informal classification of marine mammals, closely related to whales and porpoises
objects.extract: A dolphin is any one of the 40 extant species of aquatic mammal from the cetacean families Delphinidae (the oceanic dolphins), Platanistidae (the Indian river dolphins), Iniidae (the New World river dolphins), Pontoporiidae (the brackish dolphins), and the probably extinct Lipotidae (baiji or Chinese river dolphin). All these families belong to the parvorder

objects.title: Photographic film
objects.description: Visual storage media used by film cameras
objects.extract: Photographic film is a strip or sheet of transparent film base coated on one side with a gelatin emulsion containing microscopically small light-sensitive silver halide crystals, for the purpose of taking photographs. The sizes and other characteristics of the crystals determine the sensitivity, contrast, and resolution of the film.

objects.title: Multiple unit
objects.extract: A multiple-unit train (or multiple unit (MU)) is a self-propelled train composed of one or more carriages joined, and where one or more of the carriages have the means of propulsion built in. By contrast, a locomotive-hauled train has all of the carriages unpowered.

objects.title: Hay
objects.description: Dried grass, legumes or other herbaceous plants used as animal fodder
objects.extract: Hay is grass, legumes, or other herbaceous plants that have been cut and dried to be stored for use as animal fodder, either for large grazing animals raised as livestock, such as cattle, horses, goats, and sheep, or for smaller domesticated animals such as rabbits and guinea pigs. Pigs can eat hay, but they do not digest it as efficiently as herbivores do.

objects.title: Cage
objects.description: Enclosure used to confine, contain or protect something or someone
objects.extract: A cage is an enclosure often made of mesh, bars, or wires, used to confine, contain or protect something or someone. A cage can serve many purposes, including keeping an animal or person in captivity, capturing an animal or person, displaying an animal at a zoo, or protecting an object from external threats (i.e.

objects.title: Leopard
objects.description: Species of cat native to Africa and Asia
objects.extract: The leopard (Panthera pardus) is one of the five extant cat species in the genus Panthera. It has a pale yellowish to dark golden fur with dark spots grouped in rosettes.

objects.title: Doctor V64
objects.description: Development and backup device made for the Nintendo 64
objects.extract: The Doctor V64 (also referred to simply as the V64) is a development and backup device made by Bung Enterprises Ltd that is used in conjunction with the Nintendo 64. The Doctor V64 also had the ability to play video CDs and audio CDs.

objects.title: Adidas Questra
objects.description: Footballs used for major international events in the mid-1990s
objects.extract: Questra is the name given to a family of footballs originally produced by Adidas for major international events in the mid-1990s. Named after "the quest for the stars," successive versions of the ball were produced for the 1996 Olympic tournament and Euro 1996.

objects.title: Pizza
objects.extract:
Pizza is an Italian dish typically consisting of a flat base of leavened wheat-based dough topped with tomato, cheese, and other ingredients, baked at a high temperature, traditionally in a wood-fired oven.
The term pizza was first recorded in AD 997, in a Latin manuscript from the southern Italian town of Gaeta, in Lazio, on the border with Campania.

objects.title: Scooter (motorcycle)
objects.extract:
A scooter or motor scooter, is a motorcycle with an underbone or step-through frame, a seat, and a platform for the rider's feet. 
Many scooters typically have a design that focuses on an easy method of operation and fuel economy.

objects.title: Terracotta globular bottle with two handles
objects.description: bottle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 74.51.1066)

objects.title: Gratification
objects.extract: Gratification is the pleasurable emotional reaction of happiness in response to a fulfillment of a desire or goal. It is also identified as a response stemming from the fulfillment of social needs such as affiliation, socializing, social approval, and mutual recognition.

objects.title: Zither, mechanical
objects.description: instrument in the collections of the Swedish Museum of Performing Arts (M1334)

objects.title: Termite
objects.extract: Termites are a group of eusocial Cockroaches which consume a variety of decaying plant material, generally in the form of wood, leaf litter, and soil humus. They are distinguished by their beaded (moniliform) antennae and the soft-bodied, unpigmented worker caste for which they have been commonly termed "white ants"; however, they are not ants but highly der

objects.title: Dalinghosaurus longidigitus
objects.extract: Dalinghosaurus (often incorrectly spelled "Dalinghesaurus") is an extinct genus of lizards, first described in 1998 by S.A. Ji of the Peking University Department of Geology. The type species is Dalinghosaurus longidigitus.

objects.title: Sperm whale
objects.extract: The sperm whale or cachalot (Physeter macrocephalus) is the largest of the toothed whales and the largest toothed predator. It is the only living member of the genus Physeter and one of three extant species in the sperm whale superfamily Physeteroidea, along with the pygmy sperm whale and dwarf sperm whale of the genus Kogia.

objects.title: Fiat Trattori
objects.extract: Fiat Trattori S.p.A. was a Fiat group company founded in 1919 that was a constructor of agricultural equipment, tractors in particular. Over its decades of history, it established itself as Italy's leading constructor and one of the biggest in Europe; in 1991, it took over Ford-New Holland and adopted its name to increase its status on the world markets.

objects.title: Plumbing
objects.extract: Plumbing is any system that conveys fluids for a wide range of applications. Plumbing uses pipes, valves, plumbing fixtures, tanks, and other apparatuses to convey fluids.

objects.title: James Cook
objects.extract: Captain James Cook (7 November 1728 – 14 February 1779) was a British Royal Navy officer, explorer, and cartographer who led three voyages of exploration to the Pacific and Southern Oceans between 1768 and 1779. He completed the first recorded circumnavigation of the main islands of New Zealand, and led the first recorded visit by Europeans to the east coast

objects.title: McDonald's
objects.extract: McDonald's Corporation is an American multinational fast food restaurant chain. As of 2024, it is the second-largest by number of locations in the world, behind the Chinese chain Mixue Ice Cream & Tea.

objects.title: Concertina
objects.extract: A concertina is a free-reed musical instrument, like the various accordions and the harmonica. It consists of expanding and contracting bellows, with buttons (or keys) usually on both ends, unlike accordion buttons, which are on the front.

objects.title: Bulk box
objects.extract:
A bulk box, also known as a bulk bin, skid box, pallet box, bin box, gaylord, or octabin,  is a pallet-size box used for storage and shipping of bulk or packaged goods.
Bulk boxes can be designed to hold many different types of items such as plastic pellets, watermelons, electronic components, and even liquids; some bulk boxes are stackable.

objects.title: Monstera
objects.extract: Monstera is a genus of 59 species of flowering plants in the arum family, Araceae, native to tropical regions of central and south America.

objects.title: Pomacanthidae
objects.extract: Marine angelfish are perciform fish of the family Pomacanthidae. They are found on shallow reefs in the tropical Atlantic, Indian, and mostly western Pacific Oceans.

objects.title: Fluorescent lamp
objects.description: Lamp using fluorescence to produce light
objects.extract: A fluorescent lamp, or fluorescent tube, is a low-pressure mercury-vapor gas-discharge lamp that uses fluorescence to produce visible light. An electric current in the gas excites mercury vapor, to produce ultraviolet and make a phosphor coating in the lamp glow.

objects.title: Bus
objects.description: Large road vehicle for transporting people
objects.extract: A bus (contracted from omnibus, with variants multibus, motorbus, autobus, etc.) is a motor vehicle that carries significantly more passengers than an average car or van, but fewer than the average rail transport. It is most commonly used in public transport, but is also in use for charter purposes, or through private ownership.

objects.title: White-tailed Ptarmigan
objects.extract: The white-tailed ptarmigan (Lagopus leucura), also known as the snow quail, is the smallest bird in the grouse tribe. It is a permanent resident of high altitudes on or above the tree line and is native to Alaska and the mountainous parts of Canada and the western United States.

objects.title: Tram
objects.extract:
A tram (also known as a streetcar or trolley in Canada and the United States, or a tramcar) is an urban rail transit type in which vehicles, whether individual railcars or multiple-unit trains, run on tramway tracks laid on public streets, although some trams run on sections of track in their own segregated right-of-way.
Trams are a type of light rail and ar

objects.title: Broaching (metalworking)
objects.artist: Franklin Day Jones, Douglas Thomas Hamilton, Chester L. Lucas

objects.title: Brenham
objects.description: Pallasite meteorite found near Haviland, Kansas, U.S.
objects.extract: Brenham is a pallasite meteorite found near Haviland, a small town in Kiowa County, Kansas, United States.  Pallasites are a type of stony–iron meteorite that when cut and polished show yellowish olivine (peridot) crystals.

objects.title: Albatross
objects.extract: Albatrosses, of the biological family Diomedeidae, are large seabirds related to the procellariids, storm petrels, and diving petrels in the order Procellariiformes (the tubenoses). They range widely in the Southern Ocean and the North Pacific.

objects.title: Umegaemochi
objects.description: Rice cake confection from Fukuoka, Japan
objects.extract: Umegae mochi (Japanese: 梅ヶ枝餅) is a type of traditional rice cake confection originating from Dazaifu in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan.

objects.title: Togolese cuisine
objects.extract: Togolese cuisine is the cuisine of the Togolese Republic, a country in West Africa. Staple foods in Togolese cuisine include maize, rice, millet, cassava, yam, plantain and beans.

objects.title: Crown jewels
objects.description: Objects of metalwork and jewellery in the regalia of a current or former monarchy
objects.extract: Crown jewels are the objects of metalwork and jewellery in the regalia of a current or former monarchy. They are often used for the coronation of a monarch and a few other ceremonial occasions.

objects.title: Fan heater
objects.description: Heat producing machine to increase temperature of an enclosed space
objects.extract: A fan heater, also called a blow heater, is a heater that works by using a fan to pass air over a heat source (e.g. a heating element).

objects.title: Cloeon simile
objects.description: Species of insect (mayfly) found in Europe
objects.extract: Cloeon simile is a species of small minnow mayfly in the family Baetidae. It is found in Europe.
objects.artist: This image is created by user B. Schoenmakers at Waarneming.nl, a source of natu

objects.title: Furniture
objects.description: Objects used to support human activities
objects.extract: Furniture refers to objects intended to support various human activities, such as seating (e.g., stools, chairs, and sofas), eating (tables), storing items, working, and sleeping (e.g., beds and hammocks). Furniture is also used to hold objects at a convenient height for work (as horizontal surfaces above the ground, such as tables and desks), or to store th

objects.title: Waste management
objects.description: Activities and actions required to manage waste from its source to its final disposal
objects.extract: Waste management or waste disposal includes the processes and actions required to manage waste from its inception to its final disposal. This includes the collection, transport, treatment, and disposal of waste, together with monitoring and regulation of the waste management process and waste-related laws, technologies, and economic mechanisms.
objects.artist: This image was produced by me, David Castor (user:dcastor). The pictures I submi

objects.title: Puppet
objects.extract: A puppet is an object, often resembling a human, animal or mythical figure, that is animated or manipulated by a person called a puppeteer. Puppetry is an ancient form of theatre which dates back to the 5th century BC in ancient Greece.

objects.title: Lumpia
objects.description: Chinese Indonesian and Filipino spring roll
objects.extract: Lumpia (in Indonesian and Filipino) are various types of spring rolls commonly found in Indonesian and Filipino cuisines. Lumpia are made of thin paper-like or crêpe-like pastry skin called "lumpia wrapper" enveloping savory or sweet fillings.

objects.title: Bog
objects.description: Type of wetland with peat-rich soil
objects.extract: A bog or bogland is a wetland that accumulates peat as a deposit of dead plant materials – often mosses, typically sphagnum moss. It is one of the four main types of wetlands.

objects.title: dried date
objects.description: date fruit dried to remove water content

objects.title: Latham HB.5
objects.extract: The Latham HB.5 was a French biplane flying boat with four engines in push-pull configuration pairs. Ten were used by the French Navy.

objects.title: List of dairy products
objects.extract: This is a list of dairy products. A dairy product is food produced from the milk of mammals.

objects.title: Blackberry
objects.extract: The blackberry is an edible fruit produced by many species in the genus Rubus in the family Rosaceae, hybrids among these species within the subgenus Rubus, and hybrids between the subgenera Rubus and Idaeobatus. The taxonomy of blackberries has historically been confused because of hybridization and apomixis so that species have often been grouped together

objects.title: Baklava
objects.extract:
Baklava ( , or ) is a layered pastry dessert made of filo pastry, filled with chopped nuts, and sweetened with either syrup or honey.
There are several theories for the origin of this pastry, but in modern times, it is a common dessert among cuisines of countries in West Asia, Southeast Europe, Central Asia, and North Africa.

objects.title: Northwest Africa 7325
objects.extract: Northwest Africa 7325, also known as NWA 7325, is a unique igneous meteorite which crystallized as a basalt on a large asteroid or planetesimal approximately 4.56 billion years ago.  It is classified as an ungrouped achondrite, and is notable for its green fusion crust and high-magnesium/low-iron composition.

objects.title: Plant
objects.extract: Plants are the eukaryotic organisms that constitute the kingdom Plantae. They are predominantly photosynthetic, meaning that they obtain their energy from sunlight by using the green pigment chlorophyll in their chloroplasts to produce sugars from carbon dioxide and water.

objects.title: Buscaylet-de Monge 7-5
objects.extract: The Buscaylet-de Monge 7-5 was a twin engine, twin-boom aircraft without a fuselage but with pilot, passenger and fuel accommodated in a thickened wing centre-section. It was designed by Belgian pioneer Louis de Monge and built in France in the mid-1920s.

objects.title: Sid Barnes
objects.extract: Sidney George Barnes (5 June 1916 – 16 December 1973) was an Australian cricketer and cricket writer, who played 13 Test matches between 1938 and 1948. Able to open the innings or bat down the order, Barnes was regarded as one of Australia's finest batsmen in the period immediately following World War II. He helped create an enduring record when scoring 234

objects.title: Forestry
objects.description: Science and craft of managing woodlands
objects.extract: Forestry is the science and craft of creating, managing, planting, using, conserving and repairing forests and woodlands for associated resources for human and environmental benefits. Forestry is practiced in plantations and natural stands.

objects.title: Pottery in the province of Segovia
Light fixture
objects.description: Electrical device with an electric lamp
objects.extract: A light fixture (US English), light fitting (UK English) or luminaire is an electrical lighting device containing one or more light sources, such as light bulbs, and all the accessory components required for its operation to provide illumination to the environment. All light fixtures have a fixture body and one or more light sources.

objects.title: Volume
objects.extract: Volume is a measure of regions in three-dimensional space. It is often quantified numerically using SI derived units (such as the cubic metre and litre) or by various imperial or US customary units (such as the gallon, quart, cubic inch).

objects.title: Pelican
objects.description: Genus of large water birds with a throat pouch
objects.extract: Pelicans (genus Pelecanus) are a genus of large water birds that make up the family Pelecanidae. They are characterized by a long beak and a large throat pouch used for catching prey and draining water from the scooped-up contents before swallowing.
objects.artist: Mike Baird from Morro Bay, USA

objects.title: Unilever
objects.extract: Unilever PLC () is a British multinational consumer packaged goods company headquartered in London, England. It was founded in 1930 following the merger of Dutch margarine producer Margarine Unie with British soap maker Lever Brothers.

objects.title: Lander (spacecraft)
objects.extract: A lander is a spacecraft that descends towards, then comes to rest on the surface of an astronomical body other than Earth. In contrast to an impact probe, which makes a hard landing that damages or destroys the probe upon reaching the surface, a lander makes a soft landing after which the probe remains functional.

objects.title: Trunk (luggage)
objects.extract: A trunk, also known as a travel trunk, is a large cuboid container designed to hold clothes and other personal belongings. They are most commonly used for extended periods away from home, such as for boarding school, or long trips abroad.

objects.title: Roller skating
objects.description: Sport, activity, or form of transportation
objects.extract: Roller skating is the act of travelling on surfaces with roller skates. It is a recreational activity, a sport, and a form of transportation.

objects.title: Lego
objects.extract: Lego ( , LEG-oh; Danish: [ˈle̝ːko]; stylised as LEGO) is a brand of plastic construction toys manufactured by the Lego Group, a privately held company based in Billund, Denmark. Lego consists of variously coloured interlocking plastic bricks made of acrylonitrile butadiene styrene (ABS) that accompany an array of gears, figurines called minifigures, and vari

objects.title: Upper part of mummy mask and bust cartonnage. Mummification scene on the chest
objects.description: cartonnage mummy covering of the Egyptian Hellenistic period, 332–30 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy

objects.title: Washing machine
objects.extract: A washing machine (laundry machine, clothes washer, or washer) is a machine designed to launder clothing. The term is mostly applied to machines that use water.

objects.title: Jug
objects.extract: A jug is a type of container commonly used to hold and serve liquids, but not normally to drink from directly. It has an opening, sometimes narrow, from which to pour or drink, and has a handle, and usually a pouring lip.
objects.artist: The original uploader was Rodolph at English Wikipedia.

objects.title: Kettle
objects.extract: A kettle, sometimes called a tea kettle or teakettle, is a device used for heating water for tea or coffee, commonly with a lid, spout, and handle.  Strictly speaking, this refers to a stovetop kettle, which uses heat from a cooktop.

objects.title: Fountain pen
objects.description: Writing implement with nib and internal ink reservoir
objects.extract: A fountain pen is a writing instrument that uses a metal nib to apply water-based ink to paper. It is distinguished from earlier dip pens by using an internal reservoir to hold ink, eliminating the need to repeatedly dip the pen in an inkwell during use.

objects.title: Graph paper
objects.extract: Graph paper, coordinate paper, grid paper, or squared paper is writing paper that is printed with fine lines making up a regular grid. It is available either as loose leaf paper or bound in notebooks or graph books.

objects.title: Trunk (luggage)
objects.extract: A trunk, also known as a travel trunk, is a large cuboid container designed to hold clothes and other personal belongings. They are most commonly used for extended periods away from home, such as for boarding school, or long trips abroad.

objects.title: Asiatic toad
objects.extract: The Asiatic toad or Chusan Island toad (Bufo gargarizans) is a species of toad endemic to East Asia. The species was previously classified as Bufo bufo gargarizans, a subspecies of the common toad.

objects.title: MOPP (protective gear)
objects.description: Protective gear used by U.S. military personnel in a toxic environment
objects.extract: MOPP (Mission Oriented Protective Posture; pronounced "mop") is protective gear used by U.S. military personnel in a toxic environment, for example, during a chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear (CBRN) strike.

objects.title: Kabuto
objects.extract:
Kabuto (兜, 冑) is a type of helmet first used by ancient Japanese warriors that, in later periods, became an important part of the traditional Japanese armour worn by the samurai class and their retainers in feudal Japan.
Note that in the Japanese language, the word kabuto is an appellative, not a type description, and can refer to any combat helmet.

objects.title: Otamatone
objects.extract: The Otamatone (Japanese: オタマトーン, Hepburn: Otamatōn) is an electronic musical synthesizer. Shaped like an eighth note, the ribbon controller on its stem is used to control pitch, while the notes produced by the synthesizer are played from the Otamatone's "head".

objects.title: Fingerpost
objects.description: Sign post pointing in the direction of travel
objects.extract: A fingerpost (or guidepost) is a type of sign post consisting of a post with one or more arms, known as fingers, pointing in the direction of travel to places named on the fingers, often including distance information.

objects.title: Anklet
objects.extract: An anklet, also called ankle chain, ankle bracelet or ankle string, is an ornament worn around the ankle. Barefoot anklets and toe rings historically have been worn for at least over 8,000 years by girls and women in Indus Valley, in Indian subcontinent where it is commonly known as pattilu, payal, golusu and sometimes as nupur.

objects.title: Aerial firefighting
objects.description: Use of aircraft to combat wildfires
objects.extract: Aerial firefighting, also known as waterbombing, is the use of aircraft and other aerial resources to combat wildfires. The types of aircraft used include fixed-wing aircraft and helicopters.

objects.title: San Diego Zoo
objects.description: Zoo in San Diego, California, U.S
objects.extract: The San Diego Zoo is a zoo in San Diego, California, United States, located in Balboa Park. It began with a collection of animals left over from the 1915 Panama–California Exposition that were brought together by its founder, Dr.

objects.title: Gilded Mina'i Ceramic
objects.description: bottle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 57.61.6)

objects.title: High-rise (fashion)
objects.description: Clothing which sits high on or above the hips
objects.extract: A high-rise or high-waisted garment is one designed to sit high on, or above, the wearer's hips, usually at least 8 centimetres (3 inches) higher than the navel. In western cultures, high-rise jeans were especially common in the 1970s, late 1980s through the late 1990s, derided as mom jeans in the 2000s, and popular again in the mid-to-late 2010s and continu
objects.artist: iluvrhinestones from seattle, oceania, upload by Herrick 08:08, 7 December 2007

objects.title: Ultrasonic welding
objects.extract: Ultrasonic welding is an industrial process whereby high-frequency ultrasonic acoustic vibrations are locally applied to work pieces being held together under pressure to create a solid-state weld. It is commonly used for plastics and metals, and especially for joining dissimilar materials.

objects.title: Maasai people
objects.description: Ethnic group of Kenya and Tanzania
objects.extract: The Maasai () are an Eastern Nilotic ethnic group native to northern, central and southern regions of Kenya including northern Tanzania, near the African Great Lakes region. Their native language is Maa, a Nilotic language related to Dinka, Kalenjin and Nuer.

objects.title: Cookie
objects.description: Small, flat and sweetened baked food
objects.extract: A cookie is a sweet biscuit with high sugar and fat content. Cookie dough is softer than that used for other types of biscuit, and they are cooked longer at lower temperatures.

objects.title: Bee
objects.extract: Bees are winged insects that form a monophyletic clade Anthophila within the superfamily Apoidea of the order Hymenoptera, with over 20,000 known species in seven recognized families. Some species – including honey bees, bumblebees, and stingless bees – are social insects living in highly hierarchical colonies, while over 90% of bee species – including mason

objects.title: Fried rice
objects.extract: Fried rice is a dish of cooked rice that has been stir-fried in a wok or a frying pan and is usually mixed with other ingredients such as eggs, vegetables, seafood, or meat. It is often eaten as a main dish or as an accompaniment to another dish.

objects.title: Sarpedon
objects.description: 400-seater passenger boat with an open top deck and on-board catering

objects.title: Khepri
objects.extract: Khepri (Egyptian: ḫprj, also transliterated Khepera, Kheper, Khepra, Chepri) is a scarab-faced god in ancient Egyptian religion who represents the rising or morning sun. By extension, he can also represent creation and the renewal of life.

objects.title: Coffea
objects.extract: Coffea is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. Coffea species are shrubs or small trees native to tropical and southern Africa and tropical Asia.

objects.title: Squilla chydaea
objects.artist: SEFSC Pascagoula Laboratory; Collection of Brandi Noble, NOAA/NMFS/SEFSC

objects.title: Sandal
objects.description: Type of footwear with an open upper
objects.extract: Sandals are an open type of footwear consisting of a sole held to the wearer's foot by straps that go over the instep and around the ankle. Sandals may also feature a heel of varying heights.

objects.title: Diaethria eluina
objects.extract: Diaethria eluina, the eluina eighty-eight, is a species of butterfly of the family Nymphalidae. It is found from Peru to Bolivia and Brazil.

objects.title: Seat belt
objects.description: Vehicle safety device to protect against injury during collisions and sudden stop
objects.extract: A seat belt or seatbelt, also known as a safety belt, is a vehicle safety device designed to secure the driver or a passenger of a vehicle against harmful movement that may result during a collision or a sudden stop. A seat belt reduces the likelihood of death or serious injury in a traffic collision by reducing the force of secondary impacts with interior s

objects.title: Hourglass
objects.description: Device to measure the passage of time
objects.extract: An hourglass (or sandglass, sand timer, or sand clock) is a device used to measure the passage of time. It comprises two glass bulbs connected vertically by a narrow neck that allows a regulated flow of a substance (historically sand) from the upper bulb to the lower one due to gravity.

objects.title: Dry ice
objects.extract: Dry ice is the solid form of carbon dioxide. It is commonly used for temporary refrigeration as CO2  does not have a liquid state at normal atmospheric pressure and sublimes directly from the solid state to the gas state.

objects.title: Brinquedo - Trem de Luxo
objects.description: item of the collection of toys of the Museu Paulista

objects.title: Urn (cover)
objects.description: (1917.615.1.b) ceramic by Flight, Barr and Barr (British)

objects.title: Totem pole
objects.description: Monumental carvings by Indigenous peoples of the Pacific Northwest
objects.extract: Totem poles (Haida: gyáaʼaang) are monumental carvings found in western Canada and the northwestern United States. They are a type of Indigenous Northwest Coast art, consisting of poles, posts or pillars, carved with symbols or figures.

objects.title: Key
objects.description: key in the collection of the Swedish History Museum with object number 475296_HST

objects.title: Custodian helmet
objects.extract: The custodian helmet is a type of helmet worn predominantly by male police officers in the United Kingdom and within certain other places around the world. First used by the Metropolitan Police in London in 1870, the BBC labelled the custodian helmet a "symbol of British law enforcement".

objects.title: Abridor de Garrafas
objects.description: object that integrates the Museu Paulista collection (1-07-01-000-11393-00-00)
objects.artist: José Rosael/Hélio Nobre/Museu Paulista da USP

objects.title: Sprint car racing
objects.description: Auto racing with small, open-wheel, high-power vehicles
objects.extract: Sprint cars are open-wheel race cars, designed primarily for the purpose of running on short oval, circular dirt or paved tracks. Historically known simply as "big cars," distinguishing them from "midget cars," sprint car racing is popular primarily in the United States and Canada, as well as in Australia, New Zealand, and South Africa.

objects.title: Scoubidou
objects.extract: Scoubidou (Craftlace, scoobies, lanyard, gimp, or boondoggle) is material used in knotting craft. It originated in France, where it became a fad in the late 1950s and has lost popularity.

objects.title: Tricycle
objects.extract:
A tricycle, often abbreviated to trike, is a human-powered (or gasoline or electric motor-powered or assisted, or gravity-powered) three-wheeled vehicle.
Some tricycles, such as cycle rickshaws (for passenger transport) and freight trikes, are used for commercial purposes, especially in the developing world, particularly Africa and Asia.
objects.artist: One of my great aunts or uncles.

objects.title: Amboherpia heterotecta
objects.extract: Amboherpia heterotecta is a species of solenogaster within the family Acanthomeniidae. The species is found in benthic marine environments off the coasts of Norway, with the type locality being located in the Korsfjorden fjord near Bakkasund, at a depth of 610 meters.
objects.artist: Elena Gerasimova/Universitetsmuseet i Bergen, Universitetet i Bergen

objects.title: List of British cheeses
objects.extract: This is a list of cheeses from the United Kingdom.

objects.title: Lychee
objects.extract: Lychees ( LIE-chee, US also  LEE-chee) are the fruit of the tree Litchi chinensis, the sole member of the genus Litchi, part of the soapberry family, Sapindaceae. They are edible, with a sweet, mildly tart flavor, and have a distinctive floral aroma often described as rose-like.

objects.title: FOS.103
objects.description: Cheiracanthus murchisoni fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Scuba set
objects.extract: A scuba set, originally just scuba, is any breathing apparatus that is entirely carried by an underwater diver and provides the diver with breathing gas at the ambient pressure. Scuba is an anacronym for self-contained underwater breathing apparatus.

objects.title: Barometer
objects.description: Scientific instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure
objects.extract: A barometer is a scientific instrument that is used to measure air pressure. Pressure tendency, which is derived from barometric readings, can forecast short term changes in the weather.

objects.title: Aerial Distributors Distributor Wing
objects.extract: The Distributor Wing DW-1 was a prototype agricultural aircraft of unorthodox design, designed by Ken Razak in the United States and marketed by Aerial Distributors in the 1960s.

objects.title: RMS Tayleur
objects.description: Clipper ship sunk on maiden voyage in 1854
objects.extract: RMS Tayleur was a short-lived, full-rigged iron clipper ship chartered by the White Star Line. She was large, fast and technically advanced.
objects.artist: John Raphael Isaac / After C. P. Williams

objects.title: Byzantine Empire
objects.extract: The Byzantine Empire, also known as the Eastern Roman Empire, was the continuation of the Roman Empire centred on Constantinople during late antiquity and the Middle Ages. Having survived the events that caused the fall of the Western Roman Empire in the 5th century AD, it endured until the fall of Constantinople to the Ottoman Empire in 1453.

objects.title: Flex-Foot Cheetah
objects.extract:
The Flex-Foot Cheetah is a prosthetic human foot replacement developed by biomedical engineer Van Phillips, who had lost a leg below the knee at age 21; the deficiencies of existing prostheses led him to invent this new prosthesis.
The Flex-Foot Cheetah and similar models are worn by Oscar Pistorius and other amputee athletes in the Paralympics and elsewhere

objects.title: Polar bear
objects.description: Species of bear native to the Arctic
objects.extract: The polar bear (Ursus maritimus) is a large bear native to the Arctic and nearby areas. It is closely related to the brown bear, and the two species can interbreed.

objects.title: San Diego Zoo
objects.description: Zoo in San Diego, California, U.S
objects.extract: The San Diego Zoo is a zoo in San Diego, California, United States, located in Balboa Park. It began with a collection of animals left over from the 1915 Panama–California Exposition that were brought together by its founder, Dr.

objects.title: Goblet drum
objects.extract: The goblet drum (also chalice drum, tarabuka, tarabaki, darbuka, darabuka, derbake, debuka,  doumbek, dumbec, dumbeg, dumbelek, toumperleki, tumbak, tabla, or zerbaghali; Arabic: دربوكة / Romanized: darbuka) is a single-head membranophone with a goblet-shaped body. It is most commonly used in the traditional music of North Africa and the Middle East.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Arent assumed (based on copyright claims).

objects.title: Authentication
objects.extract: Authentication (from Greek: αὐθεντικός authentikos, "real, genuine", from αὐθέντης authentes, "author") is the act of proving an assertion, such as the identity of a computer system user. In contrast with identification, the act of indicating a person or thing's identity, authentication is the process of verifying that identity.

objects.title: Pizza cutter
objects.description: Tool for cutting pizza into slices
objects.extract: A pizza cutter (also known as a roller blade) is a handheld kitchen utensil that is used to cut various items into sections or slices. Due to its prevalence in the making of pizza, it has earned the name "pizza cutter".

objects.title: Broom
objects.extract: A broom (), also known as a broomstick, is a cleaning tool, consisting of usually stiff fibers (often made of materials such as plastic, glued hair, or corn husks) attached to, and roughly parallel to, a cylindrical handle, the broomstick. It is thus a variety of brush with a long handle.

objects.title: Brissopsis luzonica
objects.extract: Brissopsis luzonica is a species of sea urchins of the family Brissidae. Their armour is covered with spines.
objects.artist: Jimi N, Kimura T, Ogawa A, Kajihara H (2018) A new species of the rare, deep-sea

objects.title: Monitor lizard
objects.extract: Monitor lizards are lizards in the genus Varanus, the only extant genus in the family Varanidae. They are native to Africa, Asia, and Oceania, and one species is also found in the southern United States as an invasive species.

objects.title: Megaphone
objects.description: Portable or hand-held device to amplify voices and sounds
objects.extract: A megaphone, speaking trumpet, bullhorn, blowhorn, or loudhailer is usually a portable or hand-held, cone-shaped acoustic horn used to amplify a person's voice or other sounds and direct it in a given direction.  The sound is introduced into the narrow end of the megaphone, by holding it up to the face and speaking into it, and the sound waves radiate out th

objects.title: Jengyal bread
objects.extract: Zhingyalov hats (Armenian: Ժենգյալով հաց, also zhengyalav hatz, zhangyalov hats, or jingalov hats) is a type of flatbread stuffed with finely diced herbs and green vegetables. It is a traditional dish of Armenians from Artsakh and Syunik and has traditionally been a staple in times of scarcity, such as famine and war.

objects.title: Feather
objects.extract: Feathers are epidermal growths that form a distinctive outer covering, or plumage, on both avian (bird) and some non-avian dinosaurs and other archosaurs. They are the most complex integumentary structures found in vertebrates and an example of a complex evolutionary novelty.
objects.artist: Derek Keats from Johannesburg, South Africa

objects.title: Coin
objects.description: Small, flat and usually round piece of material used as money
objects.extract: A coin is a small object, usually round and flat, used primarily as a medium of exchange or legal tender. They are standardized in weight, and produced in large quantities at a mint in order to facilitate trade.

objects.title: Modesty
objects.description: Dress or behavior to avoid sexual attraction
objects.extract: Modesty, sometimes known as demureness, is a mode of dress and deportment which intends to avoid the encouraging of sexual attraction in others. The word modesty comes from the Latin word modestus which means 'keeping within measure'.

objects.title: Mellophone
objects.extract: The mellophone is a brass instrument used in some marching bands in place of French horns. It is a middle-voiced instrument, typically pitched in the key of F, though models in E♭, D, C, and G (as a bugle) have also historically existed.

objects.title: Alvar Aalto
objects.extract: Hugo Alvar Henrik Aalto (Finnish: [ˈhuːɡo ˈɑlʋɑr ˈhenrik ˈɑːlto]; 3 February 1898 – 11 May 1976) was a Finnish architect and designer. His work includes architecture, furniture, textiles and glassware, as well as sculptures and paintings.
objects.artist: Original uploader was Daderot at en.wikipedia

objects.title: Musée Saint-Raymond, TLFV02.1369.02
objects.description: object in the Musée Saint-Raymond of Toulouse

objects.title: List of bread dishes
objects.description: Dishes using bread as a main ingredient, listed by category
objects.extract: This is a list of bread dishes and foods, which use bread as a primary ingredient. Bread is a staple food prepared from a dough of flour and water, usually by baking.

objects.title: List of fur headgear
objects.extract: Below is a list of fur headgear (fur hats, fur caps, etc.). Some other kinds of hats can be lined or trimmed with fur, such as historical Scandinavian  karpus or kabuss/kabuds hats, or Spanish montera/montero caps.
objects.artist: Владимир Поярков; photo by George Shuklin (talk)

objects.title: Chanel
objects.extract: Chanel ( shə-NEL, French: [ʃanɛl] ) is a French luxury fashion house founded in 1910 by Coco Chanel in Paris. It is privately owned by French brothers, Alain and Gérard Wertheimer, through the holding company Chanel Limited, established in 2018 and headquartered in London.

objects.title: Model car
objects.extract: A model car, or toy car, is a miniature representation of an automobile. Other miniature motor vehicles, such as trucks, buses, and ATVs, etc.

objects.title: Digital photography
objects.extract: Digital photography uses cameras containing arrays of electronic photodetectors interfaced to an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) to produce images focused by a lens, as opposed to an exposure on photographic film. The digitized image is stored as a computer file ready for further digital processing, viewing, electronic publishing, or digital printing.

objects.title: Recycling
objects.description: Converting waste materials into new products
objects.extract: Recycling is the process of converting waste materials into new materials and objects. This concept often includes the recovery of energy from waste materials.
objects.artist: Warszawska róg Szerokiej w Tomaszowie Mazowieckim, w województwie łódzkim

objects.title: Natural rubber
objects.extract:
Rubber, also called India rubber, latex, Amazonian rubber, caucho, or caoutchouc, as initially produced, consists of polymers of the organic compound isoprene, with minor impurities of other organic compounds.
Types of polyisoprene that are used as natural rubbers are classified as elastomers.

objects.title: Coral reef
objects.description: Outcrop of rock in the sea formed by the growth and deposit of stony coral skeletons
objects.extract: A coral reef is an underwater ecosystem characterized by reef-building corals. Reefs are formed of colonies of coral polyps held together by calcium carbonate.

objects.title: Cessna 172
objects.extract: The Cessna 172 Skyhawk is an American four-seat, single-engine, high wing, fixed-wing aircraft made by the Cessna Aircraft Company. First flown in 1955, more 172s have been built than any other aircraft.

objects.title: Carbon capture and storage
objects.description: Process of capturing and storing carbon dioxide from industrial flue gas
objects.extract: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) is a process by which carbon dioxide (CO2) from industrial installations or natural sources is separated before it is released into the atmosphere, then transported to a long-term storage location. The CO2 is captured from a large point source, such as a natural gas processing plant and is typically stored in a deep geologica

objects.title: Swiss roll
objects.description: Sponge cake formed in a spiral roll, with different filling
objects.extract: A Swiss roll, jelly roll (United States), roll cake, cream roll, roulade, Swiss log, swiss cake, or jam roll or sponge roll (Australia), is a type of rolled sponge cake filled with whipped cream, jam, icing, or any type of filling. The origins of the term are unclear; in spite of the name "Swiss roll", the cake is believed to have originated elsewhere in Cen

objects.title: Mosaic
objects.description: Image made from small colored tiles
objects.extract: A mosaic () is a pattern or image made of small regular or irregular pieces of colored stone, glass or ceramic, held in place by plaster/mortar, and covering a surface. Mosaics are often used as floor and wall decoration, and were particularly popular in the Ancient Roman world.

objects.title: Hydnellum peckii
objects.extract: Hydnellum peckii is a fungus in the genus Hydnellum of the family Bankeraceae. The unusual appearance of the young fruit bodies has earned the species several descriptive common names, including strawberries and cream, the bleeding Hydnellum, the bleeding tooth fungus, the red-juice tooth, and the Devil's tooth.
